---
title: "Annotate cell types"
description: "CellTypist, scANVI, KNN label transfer."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 40
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

After [integration](integrate.qmd), each cluster is a group of cells with
similar expression — but the clusters are just numbers. Annotation assigns a
biological cell-type label to each cluster or cell.

## Two strategies

**Marker-based (manual):** inspect known marker genes per cluster (e.g. *CD3D*
for T cells, *CD19* for B cells) and assign labels by hand. Full control, but
requires domain expertise and does not scale.

**Reference-based (automated):** transfer labels from an annotated reference.
OpenPipeline supports several methods:

| Method | Workflow / component | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| CellTypist | `annotation/celltypist` | Logistic regression; large library of pretrained immune models |
| SingleR | `annotate/singler` | Correlation-based; Bioconductor reference datasets |
| PopV | `annotate/popv` | Consensus across methods; robust for diverse atlases |
| scANVI | `annotation/scanvi_scarches` | Variational autoencoder; preserves query-specific biology |
| KNN transfer | `annotation/harmony_knn`, `annotation/scvi_knn` | Direct label transfer from a reference embedding |

## Run CellTypist (no reference of your own)

CellTypist ships pretrained models for many tissues. Browse them at
[celltypist.org](https://www.celltypist.org/models).

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/annotation/celltypist/main.nf \
  --id cohort_01 \
  --input cohort_01_integrated.h5mu \
  --model "Immune_All_Low.pkl" \
  --output cohort_01_annotated.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Transfer labels from a custom reference

If you have an annotated reference atlas, transfer its labels to your query with
KNN label transfer:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/annotation/harmony_knn/main.nf \
  --id query \
  --input query_integrated.h5mu \
  --reference reference_atlas.h5mu \
  --reference_obs_targets "cell_type" \
  --output query_annotated.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Query a public atlas

Fetch cells directly from the CZ CELLxGENE Census to use as a reference:

```bash
viash run src/query/cellxgene_census/config.vsh.yaml -- \
  --tissue "brain" \
  --cell_type "neuron" \
  --output census_neurons.h5mu
```

## What comes after

With cell types assigned, common next analyses include differential expression
(the `differential_expression/pseudobulk_deseq2` workflow), RNA velocity
(`velocity/velocyto` + `velocity/scvelo`), and cell-cell communication
(`interpret/lianapy`). These are optional and depend on your biological question.

**Next:** [every annotation argument →](../reference/index.qmd)
